market_context: Markets are rallying on extended Iran ceasefire that removed binary geopolitical risk, though oil remains elevated. Strong earnings season continues with 75% of S&P 500 companies beating expectations, led by tech and energy sectors.

predictions.reasoning: QQQ's pre-market is showing at 650.05 indicating initial weakness, but the Iran ceasefire extension is driving strong risk-on sentiment with tech benefiting most from reduced geopolitical uncertainty. Broadcom and semiconductors rallied 5-8% yesterday on AI datacenter demand, and technical indicators show 'Strong Buy' signals.

Tesla reported Q1 earnings that beat on revenue ($22.4B) but warned of 'substantial' capex increases exceeding $25B in 2026, impacting free cash flow. Stock initially rose but fell 3% after Musk's guidance on elevated spending for AI and robotics initiatives.

S&P 500 futures were up 0.56% pre-market following ceasefire extension which removed major binary risk. VIX dropped from 19.62 to near 20 as volatility premium deflated. Strong earnings season with 75% of companies beating estimates supports upside momentum despite oil price concerns.

NVIDIA continues aggressive investment spree with $1B funding of Vast Data at $30B valuation, cementing AI ecosystem dominance. Despite Google's new TPU competition, NVIDIA reported strong Q4 revenue growth of 73% YoY and benefits from reduced geopolitical uncertainty driving tech rally.

GE Vernova soared 8% yesterday after beating earnings and raising 2026 guidance, driven by massive datacenter power equipment demand. Company raised revenue outlook to $44.5-45.5B with $2.4B in Q1 datacenter orders alone, more than all of 2025 combined. Momentum should continue today.
